---
title: "Publications"
description: "Full list of publications and working papers by Giacomo Lemoli"
---

## Published

::: {.pub-entry}
**Ethnic Media and the Mobilization of Identity**

Giacomo Lemoli

*Comparative Political Studies* (2025), 58(5), 890–923

::: {.pub-abstract}
<details>
<summary>Abstract</summary>
This paper studies the relationship between ethnic media, which produce content in a minority language, and the success of ethnic parties. I argue that, by embedding cultural traits in entertainment products, media outlets can shape the salience of group identity, which helps parties' mobilization efforts. I test this argument in the case of the Basque Country where, in the late phase of the Franco regime, an independent radio station operated by local clergy promoted the revival of regional language. Using contemporary, archival, and survey data, I show that exposure to ethnic radio increased support for new radical independentist parties and that the effect is driven by formerly Spanish-speaking municipalities with low historical support for Basque nationalism. I also show that radio increased bilingualism in subsequent generations and contributed to the bundling of ethnic identity revival and radical political ideology during the democratic transition.
</details>
:::

<a href="https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/00104140241252076" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Journal version</a>
<a href="https://osf.io/8rbz2/" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/PWOAK4" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Replication materials</a>
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Terrorist Attacks, Cultural Incidents, and the Vote for Radical Parties: Analyzing Text from Twitter**

Francesco Giavazzi, Felix Iglhaut, Giacomo Lemoli, and Gaia Rubera

*American Journal of Political Science* (2024), 68(3), 1002–1021

::: {.pub-abstract}
<details>
<summary>Abstract</summary>
We study the role of perceived threats from other cultures induced by terrorist attacks and criminal events on public discourse and support for radical right parties. We develop a rule which allocates Twitter users to electoral districts in Germany and use a machine learning method to compute measures of textual similarity between the tweets they produce and tweets by accounts of the main German parties. Using the exogenous timing of attacks, we find that, after an event, Twitter language becomes on average more similar to that of the main radical right party, AfD. The result is driven by a larger share of tweets discussing immigrants and Muslims, common AfD topics, and by a more negative sentiment of these tweets. Shifts in language similarity are correlated with changes in vote shares between federal elections. These results point to the role of perceived threats from minorities on the success of nationalist parties.
</details>
:::

<a href="https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/ajps.12764" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Journal version</a>
<a href="https://giacomolemoli.com/uploads/wp/Terrorist attacks/manuscript.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://giacomolemoli.com/uploads/wp/Terrorist attacks/appendix.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Online Appendix</a>
<a href="https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3550973" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Working paper version</a>
<a href="https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/VA00ZI" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Replication materials</a>
:::

---

## Working Papers

::: {.pub-entry}
**Linguistic Rights and the Borders of Political Identity**

Giacomo Lemoli (2025)

<a href="https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/3fbzh_v1" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">PDF</a>
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Collective Memories of Violence and Political Attitudes: Evidence from a World War II Frontline**

Giacomo Lemoli and Gloria Gennaro (2025)

<a href="https://osf.io/preprints/osf/983es" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://osf.io/x2gkd" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Pre-analysis plan</a>
<a href="https://www.wider.unu.edu/publication/war-violence-nationalism-and-party-support" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Outdated version</a>
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Penalties for Particularism and Partisanship? Citizens' Preferences for Legal Punishment of Clientelism**

Jeremy Horowitz, Giacomo Lemoli, and Kristin Michelitch (2025)

<a href="https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/avtqh_v1" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">PDF</a>
<a href="https://osf.io/sn4jf" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">Pre-analysis plan</a>
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Political Consequences of Natural Disasters: Evidence from a Massive Earthquake in Early Modern History**

Leonardo Baccini, Paola Giuliano, Giacomo Lemoli, and Shanker Satyanath (2025)

*Draft available upon request*
:::

---

## Work in Progress

::: {.pub-entry}
**Ethnic Bias and Partisan Bias in Kenya: Evidence from Lab and Survey Experiments**

Jeremy Horowitz, Giacomo Lemoli, and Kristin Michelitch
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Networks and Dynamics of Violent Political Mobilization: Historical Evidence from Spain**

Giacomo Lemoli and Sergi Martínez
:::

---

::: {.pub-entry}
**Measuring Group Segregation and Contact in Space Through Names, Symbols, and Pictures**

Violeta Haas and Giacomo Lemoli

*Cosmos x IHS AI-Accelerated Scholarship Program Grant*
:::
